summary: A small, oil-rich Gulf emirate with one of the world's largest sovereign wealth funds and the most assertive elected parliament in the Gulf, defined by the 1990 Iraqi invasion and US-led liberation.

history.detail: Kuwait places itself under British protection against Ottoman claims.
One of the largest oil fields on Earth; transforms the economy.
Iraq immediately claims Kuwait; British troops deter invasion.
Saddam Hussein annexes Kuwait, triggering the Gulf War.
US-led coalition expels Iraq; retreating forces set 700 oil wells ablaze.
Kuwaiti women vote and run for parliament for the first time.

finance.currency_role: Kuwaiti dinar (KWD); the world's highest-valued currency unit

resources.oil_gas: Oil ~90% of export revenue; ~6% of world reserves
resources.water: Extremely scarce; among the world's largest desalination users
resources.agriculture: Minimal; almost entirely import-dependent for food

follow_the_money.detail: Sovereign fund channels oil surpluses into global equities and real estate
10%+ of state revenue saved each year for the post-oil era

theories_disputed.detail: Debate over whether the US ambassador gave Saddam a 'green light' before the 1990 invasion.

connections.detail: Major non-NATO ally; hosts US Army's Camp Arifjan
1990 invasion; reparations and border disputes since resolved
Founding GCC partner; shared Neutral Zone oil fields
